welcome.title: Your Own AI Agent, One Click Away
welcome.desc:
Install OpenClaw quickly and easily,
then chat with AI on Telegram
welcome.policyHint: Please read the information below. Get Started stays disabled until you accept the terms.
welcome.termsAgree: I have read and agree to the <terms>Terms of Service</terms> and <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.

wslSetup.title: WSL Setup
wslSetup.notAvailable: This feature requires Windows 10 version 2004 or later.
wslSetup.checkVersion: Run winver to check your Windows version.
wslSetup.wslRequired: WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) is required to run OpenClaw.
wslSetup.autoInstall: It will be installed automatically with administrator privileges.
wslSetup.rebootDesc: A reboot is required to complete the WSL installation. Please restart OpenClaw after rebooting.
wslSetup.ubuntuInitDesc: Ubuntu is registered but needs initialization.
wslSetup.helpTitle: Need help? Use guided Windows steps (no terminal needed).
wslSetup.wslReady: WSL is ready. Proceeding to next step...
wslSetup.wslError: An error occurred during WSL installation
wslSetup.ubuntuError: An error occurred during Ubuntu installation
wslSetup.diskHintTitle: Free space on your system drive (needed for WSL)
wslSetup.diskHintBody: Ubuntu and WSL store a large virtual disk under your Windows user profile, usually on drive {{drive}}. That is separate from Settings > Storage > “New apps” on another drive. Before installing, free about {{recommendedGiB}} GiB on {{drive}} for WSL setup, Windows updates, and room to grow. Larger AI models need more space later (see the Model step).
wslSetup.diskHintFreeUnknown: Could not read free space for {{drive}}.
wslSetup.diskHintLow: Low disk space: we recommend at least {{recommendedGiB}} GiB free on {{drive}}. Try emptying Recycle Bin, removing large apps you do not need, or Disk Cleanup, then continue.

install.desc: The following items will be installed

apiKeyGuide.title: Model & Provider AI
apiKeyGuide.desc: Choose provider, enter your API key here, then pick a model

getApiKey.google: Get API Key from AI Studio

apiKeyGuide.ollamaManualMacLinuxLead: Install Ollama from the official site, or try the automated installer (Homebrew on macOS / install script on Linux). Then tap Check again so this app can detect it.
apiKeyGuide.ollamaReady: Ollama is installed. You can go to the next step.
apiKeyGuide.ollamaApiProbingBringUp: Trying port 11434 — starting Ollama in Ubuntu (WSL) if needed (can take up to ~2 min)…
apiKeyGuide.ollamaApiReady: Ollama is listening on 127.0.0.1:11434 — you can continue.
apiKeyGuide.ollamaApiBlocked: Ollama is installed in WSL but nothing is answering on port 11434 yet (Ollama’s default API port). Tap Check API below — the app will try systemctl / ollama serve in Ubuntu, then test again. Or open Ubuntu and run ollama serve yourself.
apiKeyGuide.ollamaProbeTimedOut: No response after about 2 minutes. Open Ubuntu (WSL), run ollama serve, wait a few seconds, then tap Check API again.
apiKeyGuide.ollamaProbeUnavailable: This app build is missing the API check. Fully quit Enchante, rebuild or reinstall so preload includes probeOllamaApi, then try again.
apiKeyGuide.ollamaWslLogHint: On Windows, lines like /usr/local/... come from Linux inside WSL — normal while installing. Wait until the log finishes, then tap Next.

ollamaPreflight.loadFailed: Could not read your system details. The list below still describes what you need before installing Ollama.
ollamaPreflight.title: Local models — disk, RAM, and setup
ollamaPreflight.bodyIntro: Ollama runs on your machine and downloads models (often several gigabytes each). Allow enough free space and RAM for the model you pick.
ollamaPreflight.bulletDisk: Rough guide: keep at least about {{diskMin}} GiB free on the checked volume for the Ollama install plus at least one model; large models need much more.
ollamaPreflight.bulletRam: Rough guide: at least about {{ramMin}} GiB of system RAM for smaller models; larger models are more comfortable with 16 GiB or more.
ollamaPreflight.bulletWindows: On Windows, this app uses WSL with Ubuntu. Finish the Env and WSL wizard steps first. Models are stored in Linux (usually on your Windows system drive).
ollamaPreflight.bulletMacLinux: Install Ollama from the official site or package manager, and keep the Ollama service or `ollama serve` running so OpenClaw can use 127.0.0.1:11434.
ollamaPreflight.rowDiskUnknown: Could not read free space for this path.
ollamaPreflight.wslNotReady: WSL is not ready yet. Complete the WSL step in the wizard, then use the Ollama instructions on this step.
ollamaPreflight.summaryOk: Your PC meets the rough guides above for trying Ollama (exact needs depend on the model).
ollamaPreflight.summaryWarn: Something is below the rough guide (RAM, disk, or WSL). You can still try a smaller model or free disk space first.

ollamaModelsPath.title: Store Ollama models on another drive (Windows)
ollamaModelsPath.help: Pick a folder on a drive with free space (e.g. D:\OpenClaw\Ollama). Models are saved under a subfolder named models inside WSL at /mnt/…. Leave empty and save to use the default (/root/.ollama/models on the WSL disk).
ollamaModelsPath.saved: Saved. Ollama was restarted with the new folder if systemd is available.

apiKeyGuide.oauthDesc: Sign in with your ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription

telegramGuide.title: Create a Telegram Bot
telegramGuide.desc: Let's create a Telegram bot to chat with your AI agent

steps.title: Search for BotFather
steps.desc: Search for @BotFather in the Telegram app. Select the official bot with the blue checkmark.

steps.title: Enter /newbot Command
steps.desc: Type /newbot in the BotFather chat. It will ask you for a bot name.

steps.title: Enter Bot Display Name
steps.desc: Enter a display name for your bot. Example: OPENCLAW

steps.title: Choose Bot Username
steps.desc: Enter a unique ID ending with _bot. Example: my_openclaw_bot

steps.title: Copy Bot Token
steps.desc: Done! Make sure to copy the token in the format 123456:ABCDEF...

appchatGuide.title: Chat Platform
appchatGuide.channelSubtitle: Select the chat platforms to connect with your AI agent

modelChat.title: Chat Provider
modelChat.subtitle: Zalo · Telegram · Lark — open a tab for setup help and paste tokens
modelChat.zaloOpenDocs: Zalo Bot — create bot & get token

telegramVisual.heading: Telegram — BotFather (step by step)
telegramVisual.s1d: Search for “BotFather”, open the official verified chat.
telegramVisual.s1a: Telegram search results showing BotFather with Open button
telegramVisual.s2d: In the chat, type the command /newbot to start creating a bot.
telegramVisual.s2a: Telegram chat with /newbot command and BotFather suggestion
telegramVisual.s3d: BotFather asks for a friendly name for your bot — this is not the username.
telegramVisual.s3a: BotFather message asking for the bot display name
telegramVisual.s4t: 4–5. Username ending in “bot” and copy the token
telegramVisual.s4d: Pick a username that ends with “bot” (e.g. enchantclawbot). When BotFather shows “Use this token to access the HTTP API”, copy that token and paste it in the field below.
telegramVisual.s4a: BotFather success message with username and HTTP API token area

zaloBotVisual.heading: Zalo — Zalo Bot Manager (mobile app)
zaloBotVisual.appOnly: Use the Zalo app on your phone — not the website.
zaloBotVisual.s1d: Open Zalo on your phone and search for Zalo Bot Manager (Official Account).
zaloBotVisual.s1a: Zalo search results for Zalo Bot Manager
zaloBotVisual.s2d: Open the chat and tap the button to start creating a bot.
zaloBotVisual.s2a: Zalo Bot Manager chat with Create bot button
zaloBotVisual.s3t: 3–4. Create bot account — name and introduction
zaloBotVisual.s3d: Fill display name (must start with “Bot”, min length per Zalo rules), enter an introduction about your bot (at least ~20 characters), accept terms, then create the bot account.
zaloBotVisual.s3a: Zalo create bot account form with display name and introduction fields
zaloBotVisual.s4t: 5. Copy the API token from chat
zaloBotVisual.s4d: Return to the chat with Zalo Bot Manager. In the success message, copy the HTTP API token and paste it below.
zaloBotVisual.s4a: Zalo message with bot creation success and HTTP API token

guide.zaloBot: Follow the screenshots below (Zalo app). Then paste the Bot Token. Leave empty if you skip Zalo.
guide.zaloOa: Use the Official Account (OA) integration: enter OA ID and OA Secret from the Zalo developer console (classic OA app). Leave empty if you skip this mode.
guide.telegram: Follow the BotFather steps below, then paste the token (format 123456:ABC...). Leave empty to skip Telegram.
guide.lark: In Lark/Feishu open platform, create an app, enable bot permissions, copy App ID and App Secret. Paste below. Leave empty to skip Lark.

skillsStep.title: Skills
skillsStep.subtitle: Choose ClawHub skills and bundled packs (Excel, Email, Google Workspace, …).
skillsStep.bundledHint: These copy into your OpenClaw workspace when you save configuration. Some need account details — stored only on your machine.
skillsStep.emailGmailAppPasswordHint: Gmail with 2-Step Verification: use an App password (Google Account → Security → App passwords), not your normal sign-in password.
skillsStep.emailConfigureOnAdditional: Bundled Email is enabled. Enter your mailbox address and app password on the Additional APIs step (next screen).
skillsStep.googleWorkspaceConfigureOnAdditional: Google Workspace is enabled. Follow the service account steps and attach your JSON key on the Additional APIs step (next screen).
skillsStep.emailTestAfterConfig: After you save configuration on the Config step, you can send a test email there to verify SMTP (Windows with WSL).
skillsStep.emailCredentialsTitle: Enter your email address and app password below. They are written to your local OpenClaw environment when you tap Save configuration.
skillsStep.gmailLinkHelpArticle: Google Help: Sign in with App Passwords

additionalApis.title: Additional APIs
additionalApis.subtitle: NemoClaw Shield, Google Cloud APIs, and bundled Email credentials before you review and save in Config.
additionalApis.googleSectionTitle: Google — APIs & OAuth (Calendar, Sheets, Docs, Slides, Drive)
additionalApis.googleGuideIntro: Google Cloud follows this order: enable APIs → OAuth consent screen → scopes → OAuth client ID → download credentials. Work through each step so the assistant can use Google services after you finish OpenClaw setup.
additionalApis.googleStep1Body: Select or create a project. In API Library, enable every product you need (Sheets, Docs, Slides, Drive, Calendar, Gmail, …). Enabling an API only turns it on; OAuth in the following steps is still required.
additionalApis.googleStep2Body: Configure the consent screen: User type is often External for personal use; set app name and support email. If the app is in Testing, add every Google account that will sign in under Test users.
additionalApis.googleStep3Body: Add OAuth scopes that match the APIs you enabled (e.g. Calendar events, Drive files, edit Docs/Sheets/Slides). Prefer the narrowest scopes. The Console wizard may show this as optional until you need restricted scopes.
additionalApis.googleStep4Body: Open Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID. For a local assistant on your PC, set Application type to Desktop app (this resolves “Client type is required”). Use Web application only if your integration uses HTTPS redirect URIs as documented by your skill.
additionalApis.googleStep5Body: Download the client JSON. Then follow your Google Workspace / gog skill in OpenClaw: put the file where the README says and complete its auth flow in WSL (e.g. gog auth) so the bot can call the APIs you enabled.
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaIntro: If you turned on the Google Workspace skill, add a service account key so OpenClaw can call Google APIs from scripts. Domain-wide delegation is not configured here — share individual Drive/Docs/Sheets files with the service account email when needed.
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaStep1Title: 1. Create or select a Cloud project
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaStep1Body: In Google Cloud Console, create a new project or open an existing one.
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaStep2Body: Enable each API you need: Calendar, Drive, Sheets, Slides, Docs (links below).
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaStep3Body: IAM & Admin → Service accounts → Create service account. Copy the service account email after creation.
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaStep4Body: Grant this account a project role. For quick setup, many teams use Basic → Editor; tighten roles to least privilege when you can.
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaStep5Body: Open the service account → Keys → Add key → Create new key → JSON. Store the downloaded file securely.
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaAttachLabel: Attach the service account JSON file
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaAttachHint: Pick the .json file from your computer. It must contain type "service_account" and a client_email.
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaShareNote: For files created earlier, share them in Drive (or Docs/Sheets sharing) with the new service account email so it can read or edit them.
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaInvalidJson: This file is not a valid Google service account JSON key.
additionalApis.googleWorkspaceSaConfigNote: On Save in Config, Enchante writes this key into your OpenClaw environment and refreshes workspace hints for the agent.
additionalApis.emailThenConfigSave: Continue to Config and tap Save & apply to write this setup to OpenClaw.
additionalApis.ollamaDiskGateTitle: Not enough free disk space for Ollama
additionalApis.ollamaDiskGateBody: Free space on {{volume}} is about {{freeGiB}} GiB. We recommend at least {{requiredGiB}} GiB free (estimated model download plus a safety buffer). Free some space, then tap Check again.
additionalApis.ollamaDiskGateRamHint: If you see errors about system RAM while loading a model, pick a smaller Ollama model or switch to a cloud AI provider below.

hooks.title: Nemo Claw — Skills & shield
hooks.subtitle: Optional skills install and security
hooks.nemoShieldDesc: Run OpenClaw securely inside NVIDIA OpenShell

additionalSkills.title: Additional skills
additionalSkills.subtitle: Choose bundled skills by category. Some need login details — stored only in your OpenClaw/WSL environment.
additionalSkills.empty: No bundled skills found in the app.
additionalSkills.loadError: Could not load the skill catalog.

cred.serviceAccountJson: Service account JSON (paste or attach on Additional APIs)

skillSummaries.Office Task/Google Workspace: gog + OAuth, or service account JSON (wizard) for Google APIs/scripts.

config.title: Connect & apply
config.desc: Review everything below, then apply configuration to OpenClaw
config.nemoSectionSubtitle: NemoClaw adds optional protection: when enabled, OpenClaw can run inside NVIDIA OpenShell. Turn it on to sync NemoClaw with your setup when you save configuration; leave it off if you prefer the standard environment without this layer.
config.nemoEnableLabel: Enable NemoClaw Shield (applied when you save configuration)
config.nemoShieldBeforeSaveOn: On — will be saved in this app when you tap Save.
config.nemoShieldAfterSaveOn: Active — saved in Enchante (this app’s settings).
config.nemoShieldAfterSaveOff: Not active — Enchante settings have the shield off.
config.glmSaveConfirmTitle: Download model data for this GLM profile?
config.glmSaveConfirmBody: Applying with {{modelName}} may trigger OpenClaw to download model weights or a large cache (rough estimate: {{estimate}}). You need a stable connection and enough free disk space.
config.glmFallbackAppliedNote: Applied using GLM-5 because setup with your chosen model did not finish. You can change the model later in OpenClaw.
config.ollamaInfo: Use the checklist below for RAM and disk. On Windows you need WSL + Ubuntu (earlier wizard steps). Use the step-by-step guide on this step or on Config to install **Ollama for Windows** and expose port **11434** to WSL. Switch tabs if you prefer a cloud provider.

manual.title: Install Ollama on Windows (manual steps)
manual.step01: Open ollama.com/download in your browser and download Ollama for Windows.
manual.step02: Run the installer and complete the wizard (Next / Install). When it finishes, you should see the **Ollama** icon in the system tray (near the clock).
manual.step03: Open **Ollama** from the Start menu or click the tray icon so the app is **running**. Leave it running in the background.
manual.step04: Because OpenClaw runs in **WSL**, the Ollama Windows app must accept connections from WSL on port **11434**, not only from Windows localhost. The usual fix is the **OLLAMA_HOST** system variable (substeps below).
manual.step05: Add **OLLAMA_HOST** for the whole system so WSL can reach Ollama. Follow substeps **5.1**–**5.4**; each screenshot matches one substep.
manual.step05_1: **5.1** Press **Windows**, search for **environment variables**, and open **Edit the system environment variables**.
manual.step05_2: **5.2** In **System Properties**, open the **Advanced** tab, then click **Environment Variables...**.
manual.step05_3: **5.3** Under **System variables**, click **New...** (or select **OLLAMA_HOST** and click **Edit...** if it already exists).
manual.step05_4: **5.4** Variable name: **OLLAMA_HOST**. Variable value: **0.0.0.0:11434**. Click **OK** on every dialog to save.
manual.step06: **Quit Ollama completely**: right-click the **Ollama** tray icon → **Quit** (do not only close a window). Then start **Ollama** again from the Start menu so it reads the new variable.
manual.step07: If **Windows Security / Firewall** asks for permission, allow **Ollama** or **port 11434** on **Private** networks.
manual.step08: If it still fails: **sign out of Windows** or **restart the PC**, then start Ollama again.
manual.step09: On the **Model & Provider** step in this wizard, tap **Check again** so the app can re-detect Ollama and test port 11434 from WSL.
manual.step10: For more on OLLAMA_HOST, see the Ollama networking FAQ (OLLAMA_HOST).

nothing_on_11434.title: Ollama is not responding on port 11434
nothing_on_11434.lead: Your PC did not answer on the usual local address. Install or start Ollama, then save again.
nothing_on_11434.step1: Follow steps 1–3 above (download, install, keep the Ollama app running from the tray).
nothing_on_11434.step2: If you installed from another source (portable, etc.), start Ollama so it listens on port 11434.
nothing_on_11434.step3: Alternative: in Ubuntu (WSL) run `ollama serve` and keep it running. If it fails, check `/tmp/ollama-enchante.log`.
nothing_on_11434.step4: Return here and tap Save configuration again.
nothing_on_11434.noteNoWinExe: We did not find Ollama.exe in the usual Windows folders — a normal install from ollama.com usually fixes this.

bind_for_wsl.title: Ollama is on Windows but WSL cannot reach it
bind_for_wsl.lead: Often the Windows app only listens on 127.0.0.1. WSL needs Ollama to listen on all interfaces.
bind_for_wsl.step2: Set **OLLAMA_HOST** to **0.0.0.0:11434** using **System variables** as in step 5 above. Sign out of Windows or reboot if the change does not apply.
bind_for_wsl.step3: Start Ollama again, allow port 11434 in Windows Firewall if asked, then Save configuration again.

try_windows_host.title: Start the Ollama app on Windows
try_windows_host.lead: We will use your Windows PC’s address from inside WSL. The Ollama desktop app must be running.
try_windows_host.step1: Open Ollama from the system tray. If it is not installed yet, start from step 1 above.

config.zaloBotTokenPlaceholder: Paste Bot Token from Zalo message
config.zaloModeConflict: Use either Bot token or OA ID + Secret — not both.
config.zaloOaIncomplete: Enter both OA ID and OA Secret, or switch to Bot token.
config.zaloPairingSaveFirst: Tap Save to unlock Zalo pairing below. You don’t have to approve a code before continuing — you can pair on the final screen while Gateway runs.
config.applyDoneHint: Configuration applied. Press Next to open the completion screen.
config.ollamaTechnicalReportFoot: You do not need a terminal — this is the same report Enchante collected while saving. Share it with support if something still fails.
config.logHintClawHub: Red “Rate limit exceeded” lines come from ClawHub while installing skills — the installer waits and retries until each skill shows OK. That is normal, not a failed setup.
config.logHintChannelSecurity: In the Channels tab, the Fixer “Security” block about Zalo pairing is a policy reminder (who may message the bot). It is not an error if Zalo lines show “polling loop started”.
config.emailBundledIntro: Required because you enabled the Email skill. Stored only on your machine when configuration is applied.
config.emailTestWindowsOnly: Sending a test email from the app requires Windows with WSL (same environment as OpenClaw). On other systems, use the skill’s CLI or check ~/.config/imap-smtp-email/.env after setup.
config.emailFlowSteps: Order: (1) Your mailbox address (2) Gmail App Password — 16 characters (3) Save once, then use Apply if you change password (4) Enter recipient and Send test.
config.emailGmailAppPasswordExampleAlt: Google Account: Generated app password screen (16-character app password, not your email sign-in password)
config.emailGmailAppPasswordExampleCaption: This is what a Google App password looks like. Paste that 16-character code below — not your normal Gmail password.
config.emailThenTapSave: Fill both fields above, then tap Save configuration below.
config.emailApplyCredsHint: If you just created an App Password in Google, paste it in the field above, tap Apply email settings, then send the test again.
config.emailApplyCredsBtn: Apply email & App Password to OpenClaw
config.emailTestRecipientSubtitle: Enter who should receive the test message (can be the same as your address).

portStatus.gwRunningNoPort: Gateway is running but not using the port
